one_time_description: Correction of an error reducing opening accumulated surplus by $785,035 (Note 22)
economic_notes: First Nation holds a 1/7 ownership interest in the South Beach Group of Partnerships (casino and hotel on Brokenhead Ojibway Nation land). Also a 12.5% beneficial owner of Shawano Wapunong Investments Corp. which invests in commercial real estate in Winnipeg. Demand loan of up to $4,669,000 taken to pursue legal claim against Government of Canada.
notes: Fiscal year is 2021, not 2024. Audit includes Emphasis of Matter paragraph regarding demand loan payable taken to pursue legal claim against Government of Canada (max $4,669,000). Prior year figures are restated due to correction of an error (Note 22) reducing opening surplus by $785,035. Black River Development Corporation is fully consolidated. South Beach Group of Partnerships accounted for by modified equity method (1/7 interest). First Nation is not in compliance with CMHC replacement reserve requirements. OSR is negative because revenue deferred to subsequent year exceeds non-government revenue sources; total government transfers calculated as ISC $14,946,374 + CMHC $365,109 + Assembly of Manitoba Chiefs $621,717 + Manitoba Justice $58,000 = $15,991,200, with net deferred revenue adjustments making OSR effectively negative. expenses_housing combines Housing ($520,560) and Subsidized Housing ($708,063). expenses_salaries_total taken from cash flow statement cash paid to employees.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
High ISC dependency (98.44%) — operations heavily reliant on federal transfers
